title: Editor Medical Communications
description:
Editor

Accelerating life-changing solutions to global healthcare challenges is the vision that unites us through a shared sense of purpose, whatever the role.

It speaks of the significant challenges that exist within global healthcare, and how we, as one team, can help to deliver better solutions and outcomes for our partners and for patients everywhere, sooner.

Importantly too is how we go about living our vision. This is defined by the Prime mission:

Through a pioneering fusion of the finest expertise in science, strategy, evidence, technology and creativity we deliver the world-class outcomes that our global partners trust to achieve their goals.

It speaks about collaboration and everyone's responsibility to bring together the diverse expertise that exists across our team to deliver world-class outcomes.

On this foundation, we have defined four core values, these represent the shared principles that guide our behaviors every day, whatever your role:

Be Brave, Be Human, Be Passionate, Be Exceptional.

At Prime these values are integral to who we are. They are there to guide you in your role and support you on your personal journey to success. They shape our culture and support us in achieving our vision together, as one global team.

The Role

Our Editors review and edit a wide range of content outputs (including manuscripts, abstracts, posters, slides, PDFs, and digital materials) for grammar, spelling, consistency, and clarity while ensuring alignment with house, client and journal style guides. They apply consistent and appropriate use of digital markup tools, tracked changes and comments to provide clear, actionable editorial feedback.

They contribute to effective and timely project delivery by performing dummy submissions, ensuring the team is aware of specific requirements related to journal and congress guidelines. They proactively communicate and collaborate with internal teams to ensure best practices are being followed.

The right candidate will have impeccable English spelling, punctuation and grammar skills, and will display meticulous attention to detail.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Reviewing and editing a wide range of content outputs (including manuscripts, abstracts, posters, slides, PDFs, digital materials) for grammar, spelling, consistency, and clarity.
  • Ensuring alignment with house, client and journal style guides.
  • Applying consistent and appropriate use of digital markup tools, tracking changes and comments to provide clear, actionable editorial feedback.
  • Proofing typeset copy to include consideration of layout (figures/tables, colors, size, quality, and resolution) and liaise with studio and/or journal for necessary amends.
  • Checking redrawn figures, PowerPoint and PDFs from studio to ensure accuracy versus submitted materials.
  • Working with the teams to ensure all required copyright and author permissions are in place, and the relevant copyright wording is included for reproduced materials.
  • Owning and updating client style guides; ensures consistent application across accounts
  • Verifying content for scientific accuracy and consistency by verifying data, references, and source materials in line with SOPs.
  • Collaborating with the scientific team to resolve data discrepancies or uncertainties.
  • Escalating concerns or unclear data to relevant team members to ensure final outputs meet Prime quality standards.
  • Recording and monitoring actual vs. budgeted time on projects and completing timesheets accurately each week, submitting by the deadline.
  • Proactively flagging when task scope, timelines, or budgets are misaligned.

Requirements

  • Life sciences degree required
  • 1+ year of experience working for a medical communications company, medical journal or similar
  • Highest standards of English spelling, punctuation, and grammar
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Solutions-focused, sound judgement and problem-solving abilities
  • Ability to effectively work in a fast-paced agency setting
  • Strong multi-tasking, planning and organizational skills
  • Proficiency in Word, PowerPoint, Excel, Adobe Acrobat, EndNote, Outlook, Teams and SharePoint
  • Experience with digital markup, tracked changes, and editing tools
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • Openness to learning, feedback and career development

Benefits

  • Supplemental medical, dental and vision insurance plans
  • Competitive salary
  • Generous time off
  • Pension
  • 401k Plan with match
  • Health & Wellbeing offerings
  • Job training and advancement opportunities

title: Engineer II
description:
Company Description
Work with Us. Change the World.
At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the worldâs most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations.

There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world.

We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us.

Job Description
For New Project Jodhpur

Qualifications
Additional Information
At AECOM, we are committed to maintaining a secure and trustworthy recruitment process and take any fraudulent hiring activity seriously. To support this commitment, all newly hired employees are required to attend an in-person Day 1 onboarding at an AECOM office location as a condition of employment.
About AECOM
AECOM is proud to offer comprehensive benefits to meet the diverse needs of our employees. Depending on your employment status, AECOM benefits may include medical, dental, vision, life, AD&D, disability benefits, paid time off, leaves of absences, voluntary benefits, perks, flexible work options, well-being resources, employee assistance program, business travel insurance, service recognition awards, retirement savings plan, and employee stock purchase plan.

AECOM is the global infrastructure leader, committed to delivering a better world. As a trusted professional services firm powered by deep technical abilities, we solve our clientsâ complex challenges in water, environment, energy, transportation and buildings. Our teams partner with public- and private-sector clients to create innovative, sustainable and resilient solutions throughout the project lifecycle â from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm that had revenue of $16.1 billion in fiscal year 2025. Learn more at aecom.com.

What makes AECOM a great place to work
You will be part of a global team that champions your growth and career ambitions. Work on groundbreaking projects - both in your local community and on a global scale - that are transforming our industry and shaping the future. With cutting-edge technology and a network of experts, youâll have the resources to make a real impact. Our award-winning training and development programs are designed to expand your technical expertise and leadership skills, helping you build the career youâve always envisioned. Here, youâll find a welcoming workplace built on respect, collaboration and community - where you have the freedom to grow in a world of opportunity.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in your potential and are here to help you achieve it. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

title: Director of Customer Experience
location: New York, New York, New York, United States
description:
About us

LifeMD is a leading provider of virtual primary care, telehealth, and specialized treatment programs serving hundreds of thousands of patients nationwide. Our vertically integrated platform combines 50-state licensed providers, in-house pharmacy and lab integrations, and proprietary technology that enables safe, high-quality, and affordable care.

At the heart of this transformation is our team of developers, engineers, and tech innovators building state-of-the-art systems that make healthcare smarter, faster, and more accessible. From architecting scalable backend systems to crafting intuitive user experiences, we are pushing boundaries every day.

Recognized as one of the fastest-growing healthcare tech companies (#166 on Deloitte Fast 500 in 2023), LifeMD is not just a healthcare company â it's a tech company revolutionizing healthcare.

About the role

LifeMD is seeking a strategic, data-obsessed Director of Customer Experience to lead and elevate our remote customer operations. This role sits at the intersection of technology, clinical empathy, and operational excellence. We are looking for someone who has built and continuously evolved high-performing, technology-enabled CX organizations; deploying AI voice and chat tools, engineering data-driven performance cultures, and creating differentiated, concierge-level experiences that drive loyalty and growth.

Reporting directly to the SVP of Operations & Patient Experience, you will translate the executive team's vision and strategy into operational reality,surfacing the voice of the customer, driving measurable performance, and partnering cross-functionally to embed customer insights into decisions across the organization. If you thrive in fast-moving health tech environments and believe exceptional customer experience is a commercial advantage, we want to hear from you.

Responsibilities

Remote Contact Center Leadership

  • Lead, scale, and continuously optimize a remote contact center team across multiple channels (voice, chat, email, SMS). Setting KPIs, driving performance expectations, and driving continuous improvement.
  • Set and enforce service level standards, workforce management practices, scheduling, capacity models, and escalation protocols
  • Build a high-accountability, coaching-first culture across team leads and frontline agents

Customer Experience

  • Develop and maintain service blueprints and experience standards across contact and service touchpoints that transform support from a transactional function into a driver of loyalty and advocacy
  • Champion a culture of empathy, accountability, and customer-centricity across the entire CX organization

AI & Technology Integration

  • Own the performance of AI-powered support functions including AI voice (conversational IVR), chatbots, and agent-assist tools â monitoring deflection rates, containment quality, sentiment signals, and escalation patterns
  • Partner with Product and Engineering to evaluate, implement, and iterate on AI tooling that augments agent performance and improves resolution rates without sacrificing human connection
  • Ensure deep integration between CX technology and broader customer-facing platforms including EHR, CRM, and marketing automation systems
  • â¢ Stay current on emerging customer support technologies and serve as the internal subject matter expert on responsible AI use within a healthcare context

Data-Driven Performance Management

  • Define, track, and improve core CX metrics: first contact resolution, average handle time, CSAT, NPS, escalation rate, AI containment rate, retention, and reactivation
  • Monitor NPS trends surfaced through the CX function and partner with the Manager of Patient Experience to close the loop on customer feedback and drive operational improvements
  • Build and maintain dashboards and reporting cadences that provide real-time operational visibility to your team and to the SVP
  • Conduct root cause analysis on performance gaps and translate findings into structured improvement plans
  • Synthesize customer insights, VOC data, and operational trends into clear, executive-ready briefings

Commercial Impact & Customer Lifecycle

  • Align CX operations with revenue and retention goals, connecting CSAT, NPS, and resolution metrics to commercial outcomes including retention, upsell conversion, and reactivation
  • Identify patterns in customer friction that represent risk to renewal, referral, or acquisition, and advocate for fixes cross-functionally
  • Support onboarding and activation workflows that reduce time-to-value; contribute to behavioral engagement and churn prediction strategies

Quality Assurance

  • Design and maintain a rigorous QA framework covering interaction quality, compliance, empathy standards, and clinical accuracy
  • Establish calibration processes across QA reviewers to ensure scoring consistency and fairness
  • Use QA insights to inform targeted coaching, training curricula, and process improvement

Budget Management

  • Manage the CX department budget within parameters, including headcount planning, vendor contracts, and technology spend
  • Identify cost optimization opportunities without compromising service quality or customer outcomes; present trade-off analyses and recommendations

Remote Transition & Operational Build-Out

  • Lead the operational transition to a fully remote customer service model, owning the end-to-end migration across people, process, technology, and compliance workstreams
  • Partner with HR, IT, Legal, and Operations to execute the transition with minimal disruption to the customer experience, establishing remote workforce policies and tooling standards
  • Use the transition as an opportunity to redesign foundational processes and eliminate legacy inefficiencies

Team Leadership & Development

  • Recruit, develop, and retain a high-performing team of managers, team leads, QA specialists, trainers, and agents
  • Build workforce planning, career pathing, and succession pipelines within the CX organization
  • Design the management rhythms, coaching frameworks, and recognition programs that attract and retain top talent in a fully remote environment

Cross-Functional Partnership & Internal Alignment

  • Serve as a key operational partner to the Manager of Patient Experience, ensuring alignment between customer-facing service operations and the broader patient experience program
  • Collaborate closely with the Manager of Patient Experience to align on messaging touchpoints, technology integrations, and shared data â recognizing that the customer journey spans both functions and requires active coordination to deliver a coherent experience
  • Support the SVP of Operations & Patient Experience in executing the overall CX vision, roadmap, and resource planning
  • Collaborate with Product, Clinical, Marketing, Sales, and Finance to embed the customer perspective in upstream decisions
  • Prepare structured insights, trend analyses, and investment recommendations for the SVP to carry into executive forums

Requirements

  • 7+ years of progressive CX, contact center, or customer operations leadership experience, with direct people management responsibility
  • Demonstrated experience leading remote or distributed teams of 50 or more across multiple channels and functions
  • Hands-on experience implementing or managing AI/automation in a customer service context (chatbots, conversational IVR, agent-assist tools)
  • Strong analytical skills â comfortable building dashboards, interpreting performance data, and translating insights into operational action
  • Proven track record connecting CX performance to commercial outcomes including retention, reactivation, and revenue
  • Experience managing vendor relationships including BPO partners and technology providers
  • Experience building or maturing QA programs in a regulated or compliance-sensitive environment
  • Demonstrated ability to manage a department budget, including headcount, vendor, and technology spend

Preferred

  • Background in health tech, digital health, telehealth, or healthcare services
  • Familiarity with HIPAA requirements as they apply to CX operations, technology platforms, and data handling
  • Experience with platforms such as Salesforce Service Cloud, Zendesk, Five9, Genesys, or similar CRM/WFM tools
  • Exposure to customer and patient experience frameworks (e.g., service blueprinting, NPS program design, churn prediction models)
  • Background in a direct-to-consumer or subscription-based business where CX plays a direct role in lifetime value

Benefits

  • Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental & Vision)
  • Retirement Plans
  • Life Insurance (Basic, Voluntary & AD&D)
  • Flexible PTO Policy
  • Paid Holidays
  • Short Term Disability
  • Training & Development

title: Creative Director
description:
About The Opportunity

LMG Staffing Solutions is partnering with a collaborative, design-driven digital agency to identify a Creative Director for a contract opportunity based in Philadelphia.

This role brings together brand strategy, creative leadership, and digital expertise to help clients solve complex business challenges through compelling, human-centered experiences. You will lead the strategic and creative vision across a diverse portfolio that may include healthcare, higher education, enterprise organizations, and emerging brands.

The ideal candidate can guide engagements from discovery through execution, lead multidisciplinary teams, strengthen creative quality, and connect creative recommendations to measurable business outcomes.

What You'll Do

Strategic Brand Leadership

  • Lead the development of brand strategies, positioning, messaging frameworks, and digital experience recommendations
  • Connect strategic and creative recommendations to client goals, audience insights, and business objectives
  • Facilitate discovery sessions, stakeholder workshops, and strategic planning engagements
  • Translate research and business insights into compelling brand narratives, messaging platforms, and customer experience strategies
  • Identify opportunities across marketing, digital experiences, products, and services
  • Partner with client services teams to align recommendations with client priorities, budgets, and long-term goals

Creative Leadership

  • Establish the strategic and creative vision for client engagements
  • Develop creative briefs with clear direction, measurable objectives, and actionable guidance
  • Lead multidisciplinary teams across branding, web, UX/UI, content, digital marketing, and campaign development
  • Provide thoughtful creative direction and feedback that strengthens ideas and maintains brand integrity
  • Ensure work is strategically grounded, human-centered, and aligned with business outcomes
  • Mentor designers, writers, strategists, and other creative team members
  • Help foster a collaborative, accountable, and high-performing creative culture

Client Partnership

  • Serve as a trusted strategic advisor to executive stakeholders and marketing leaders
  • Present creative concepts, brand strategies, and campaign recommendations with confidence
  • Facilitate conversations that connect business goals with creative opportunities
  • Build alignment across client stakeholders and internal teams
  • Collaborate with account and project management teams to support successful delivery while protecting strategic and creative integrity

Research & Strategic Planning

  • Lead stakeholder interviews, audience research, competitive analysis, surveys, and workshop facilitation
  • Synthesize complex information into clear recommendations and executive-ready presentations
  • Apply design thinking and human-centered problem-solving to uncover meaningful insights
  • Monitor trends in branding, customer behavior, digital experiences, marketing, technology, and AI-enabled solutions
  • Contribute strategic and creative thinking to new business opportunities

Requirements

What We're Looking For

  • 5+ years of experience leading brand, digital, or integrated creative initiatives within an agency or consulting environment
  • Proven experience developing brand strategies, creative campaigns, digital experiences, and integrated marketing initiatives
  • Strong understanding of branding, UX/UI, web design, digital marketing, content strategy, and customer experience
  • Experience leading multidisciplinary teams across strategy, design, content, marketing, and technology
  • Exceptional storytelling, facilitation, presentation, and executive communication skills
  • Ability to translate complex business challenges into simple, compelling creative solutions
  • Experience presenting to executive leadership and facilitating strategic workshops
  • Strong understanding of modern digital ecosystems, including websites, CMS platforms, paid media, marketing automation, analytics, and emerging AI-enabled experiences
  • Collaborative leadership style grounded in curiosity, empathy, accountability, and continuous learning

Why This Role

  • Contract opportunity with a collaborative, design-driven agency
  • Lead strategic and creative work across diverse client engagements
  • Shape brand strategies, digital experiences, and integrated campaigns
  • Partner directly with executive stakeholders and marketing leaders
  • Mentor multidisciplinary creative teams and elevate creative standards
  • Contribute to new business growth and agency thought leadership
  • Balance creative excellence with measurable business impact

If you are a strategic creative leader who enjoys building brands, guiding teams, and shaping digital experiences that move organizations forward, we would love to connect.

User Experience Designer

Full-time

LOCATION

Remote and HQ in Edinburgh when required

Overview

Weâre looking for a UX Designer who thrives on solving complex problems and making digital experiences feel effortless. You care about clarity, usability, and reducing friction â not just shipping pretty screens.

This role sits at the heart of how users engage with Caledonia. Youâll shape flows, build systems, and help turn financial services into something people actually enjoy using.

If you think like a designer but move like a product builder, we want to talk.

What You'll Do

  • Design end-to-end user experiences across web and mobile products
  • Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to shape feature direction and UX priorities
  • Translate product requirements into wireframes, prototypes, and detailed visual designs
  • Run user research, usability testing, and iterate based on real feedback
  • Develop and maintain UX patterns and documentation to ensure consistency
  • Help define and evolve the design language and component library
  • Present design decisions clearly and advocate for user needs in every conversation

What You'll Bring

  • 3 or more years of experience in UX, interaction design, or product design
  • A strong portfolio that shows how you solve real-world problems through design
  • Proven experience designing digital products, not just landing pages or marketing sites
  • Excellent understanding of design tools like Figma, along with prototyping and handoff workflows
  • Comfortable working alongside engineers and product managers in agile teams
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to explain design thinking clearly
  • A mindset of constant learning, with a sharp eye for detail and improvement

About Caledonia

Caledonia is a next-generation financial company focused on serving freelancers, creators, and independent professionals. Weâre building adaptive, AI-powered banking infrastructure that challenges the legacy systems of the past.

We are not yet a licensed EMI or bank. That is part of what we are building, together.

Who Youâll Work With

This role reports to the Head of Product and works closely with engineers, fellow designers, and Caledoniaâs co-founders. Youâll be part of the core team shaping our user experience from day one.

Apply Now

Think youâre a fit? Use the form below to send us your CV and a short note about why youâre interested. We review every application personally.

Weâre reviewing applications on a rolling basis. Expect a quick, human response.

title: Marketing Assistant
description:
Venture Media is a full-service content marketing agency. We help businesses grow through social media, podcast production, SEO and AI visibility, and full-funnel content systems. We are a small, fast-moving remote team that ships real client work every week.


The role

We are looking for a Marketing Assistant to support content production and daily execution across our client accounts and internal brands. You will keep the content engine running: scheduling posts, prepping assets, and running research. Your job will be across assistant and marketing work, so you should like variety.


Marketing expertise is a plus for this role, but it is not required. What matters most is having a basic understanding of marketing and a genuine desire to learn and develop deeper expertise. We use automation and clear systems for the heavy lifting, so your job is less about busywork and more about keeping things organized, checking quality, and helping our content and clients look great. You will work directly with the founder and learn how a modern agency runs end to end.


What you will do

  • Content coordination and publishing. Schedule and publish blogs and posts, keep our content calendar organized, and make sure everything goes out on time. Much of the writing is already automated, so your role is organizing, reviewing, and adding the final human touch.
  • Quality control. Do a first pass on assets from our editors, proofread copy and decks before they go to clients, and make sure feedback actually gets addressed.
  • Research and reporting. Run market research for projects as needed, and help pull simple analytics reports so we know what is working.
  • Social and community support. Help manage engagement and distribution across our platforms, and keep our own brand active and consistent.
  • Light marketing execution. Learn the basics of SEO and AI search visibility, and help apply them to our content. We will teach you this piece by piece.
  • General right-hand work. Jump on whatever the day needs. Some days that is research, some days it is organizing a project in our task tools, some days it is prepping something for a client.


What we are looking for

  • Bachelor's in a related field or equivalent hands-on experience
  • 1 to 2 years in a marketing, social media, communications, or administrative role, ideally at an agency 
  • Extremely organized and proactive, able to juggle multiple priorities and stay detailed under deadline
  • Comfortable with the major social platforms (Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn, YouTube) and scheduling tools
  • Familiar with project management software
  • A reliable communicator who keeps managers updated and follows through without being chased
  • A flexible team player who adapts to shifting priorities and jumps in where needed
  • Clear written English and comfort in an async remote setup
  • Available during US Pacific business hours so we can work in real time
  • Dedicated to this as your main full-time focus, not a side gig


Nice to have (not required)

  • SEO or AI search visibility experience
  • Experience with task managers like ClickUp


Who this is not for

This is not a fit if you need constant hand-holding, avoid feedback, or prefer slow work with a lot of meetings. We move fast, hold clear standards, and expect you to own your tasks.


What you get

  • An hourly rate of $20 to $25 USD, based on your experience.
  • Direct mentorship from the founder and a front-row seat to how a marketing agency runs
  • Real room to grow. As we grow, you grow, in both responsibility and pay
  • A long-term home, not a short-term contract


How to apply

Submit your resume and a short note about yourself, and answer this one question: what is one thing you would most want to get better at in this role, and why?


We read every application that answers it.

title: Backend Developer
description:
About BJAK

The original mission of BJAK is we believe people deserve smarter ways to plan, save and grow their money. This is the origin of our name.

Started in 2019, we built the first mobile-first, insurance platform, enabling insurance to be accessible online by millions in the region. Today, its the leading insurance platform in Southeast Asia.

Today, we are expanding ways to help people in the region â this includes spending, saving, investing, exchanging, travelling, and more. Our mission is help people get more from their money every day.

We have teams working around the world, with over 20 nationalities from our offices and remotely, who truly enjoys their work. We are looking for the most talented and driven people we can find. We are looking for people who work for their passion, not counting hours. Who loves building great next-generation products, not status quo. Who cares about redefining how everyone around us can get the best financial applications, not for an exclusive few.

If you're this person, we'd love to talk to you.

Â 

The Role

We are looking for backend engineers to build the core systems behind BJAK's AI Neobank app.

This role is for engineers who can build reliable APIs, workflows and financial product infrastructure that can scale across markets, partners and millions of users.

Â 

What You'll Own

  • Build backend services and APIs for onboarding, accounts, payments, cards, transfers, rewards, travel, FX, KYC and risk flows.

  • Design reliable systems that can support real customer transactions, operational workflows and future financial products.

  • Integrate with internal systems, financial partners, payment providers, AI services and third-party platforms.

  • Own backend quality across reliability, security, latency, observability, data integrity and incident handling.

  • Work with product, frontend, mobile and AI teams to launch features quickly and safely.

  • Improve existing systems by simplifying flows, removing manual work and automating repeatable processes.

    Â 

What We're Looking For

  • Strong backend engineering experience in production systems.

  • Good understanding of APIs, databases, authentication, authorization, queues, jobs, events and integrations.

  • Experience building services used by real customers or internal operations at meaningful scale.

  • Strong debugging, reliability, security and data quality mindset.

  • Able to make practical trade-offs between speed, correctness, scalability and maintainability.

  • Fintech, payments, banking, wallet, KYC, fraud or risk experience is a strong plus, but not required if fundamentals are strong.

Â 

This Role Is Not For

  • Engineers who only want isolated backend tasks.

  • People who ignore security, data consistency, monitoring or operational risk.

  • Developers who cannot work with fast-changing product requirements.

  • Candidates who talk about scale but have not handled production systems.

  • People who exaggerate impact without being clear on ownership.


The Bar

We are looking for engineers with real technology career highlights. A strong highlight is not just working at a famous company. A strong highlight means you personally helped build, ship or scale a serious product, platform or system.

Â 

Location

This role is remote, but candidates must be based in India. We are hiring specifically for this market, so applicants should already be based in India.

Â 

Language

English is our main working language across global teams. Strong English communication is required.

Â 

Interview Process

Our process is designed to move fast:

1. Online assessment or practical task

2. Technical interview

3. CEO / final round


For strong candidates, we aim to complete the process and make an offer within 1 week from the start of the interview process. Candidates who complete assessments quickly will be prioritized.

title: Performance Analyst
company: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
description:
Bristol, Darlington, London, Manchester, Wolverhampton

Job Summary

We are MHCLG

Here at the Ministry of Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG), we work on things that make a real difference to peopleâs lives. Whether it's through the homes we live in, the work of our local councils, or the communities weâre all part of, our work is at the top of the political agenda. We have ambitious and far-reaching outcomes to achieve, so thereâs never been a more exciting time to join us.

This Performance Analyst role will be part of our Elections Digital team at MHCLG. Itâs an exciting time to improve and expand our services to meet usersâ needs.

Our Services Include

  • Register to vote
  • Apply for a proxy vote
  • Apply for a postal vote
  • Apply for photo ID to vote (called a âVoter Authority Certificateâ)
  • And a product we have built for electoral teams in Local Authorities to support electoral administration.

We have lots to do. We must continue to improve our existing services to meet the needs of our users, especially those of disabled and other marginalised users. We must also think strategically about the future, understanding what problems still exist and champion user-centred ways of working to approach these issues.

You will be embedded in the Operations team, providing statistics and analysis for an existing portfolio of high-profile online services. Moreover, a substantial part of your responsibilities will be working with the Product and Design teams in Elections Digital, who are busy developing entirely new and exciting online services to improve voter registration as part of the Electoral Resilience & Democratic Engagement Programme.â¯â¯

This role will involve working closely with the development team on supporting user research and various analytics for services in various stages of development, and also performing analytics and continuous improvement to our existing services.â¯â¯

The role will also focus on supporting improvements to our digital compliance, making sure that we have proportionate data governance across the operational portfolio of live services that is aligned with MHCLG and data governance frameworks.

Find out more about our Digital teams and what they are working on through our MHCLG Digital blog. Please note that MHCLG do not offer visa sponsorship and applicants will need a valid visa for the duration of your employment.

â¯

Job Description

As a Performance Analyst, you'll:â¯â¯â¯

  • Be responsible for the performance analysis in your given area and help the product team measure and assess outcomes: for example, what good looks like and how it will be measured
  • Help to plan and ensure project delivery, finding opportunities to share information with the product team, community colleagues and others. Also to integrate the use of data analytics across the different phases of product of the product lifecycle
  • Be adaptable in applying analysis techniques to new contexts, especially when acting as the sole representative for performance analysts on a project
  • Be able to work on data governance artifacts with the wider team, including identifying documentary requirements, and then taking responsibility as a subject matter expert to deliver against these requirements
  • Be responsible for the accuracy and quality of data and analysis and how they are used, including putting robust quality assurance in place
  • Be engaged with, and actively contribute to, the performance analysis community

As a Performance Analyst, you'll:â¯â¯â¯

  • Be responsible for the performance analysis in your given area and help the product team measure and assess outcomes: for example, what good looks like and how it will be measured
  • Help to plan and ensure project delivery, finding opportunities to share information with the product team, community colleagues and others. Also to integrate the use of data analytics across the different phases of product of the product lifecycle
  • Be adaptable in applying analysis techniques to new contexts, especially when acting as the sole representative for performance analysts on a project
  • Be able to work on data governance artifacts with the wider team, including identifying documentary requirements, and then taking responsibility as a subject matter expert to deliver against these requirements
  • Be responsible for the accuracy and quality of data and analysis and how they are used, including putting robust quality assurance in place
  • Be engaged with, and actively contribute to, the performance analysis community

Person specification

As a Performance Analyst, You'll Have

  • Knowledge on how to use the most appropriate medium to visualise data to tell compelling and actionable stories relevant for business goals. You can present, communicate and disseminate analysis and recommendations effectively, appropriately and with high impact
  • Knowledge on how to enable the team to develop a performance framework including KPIs or a service or product. You can provide the intelligence to support the formulation of both short-term and long-term strategic plans. You understand the different phases of product delivery and can plan and run the analysis for these. You can contribute to decision-making throughout the product life cycle
  • Skills to develop subject matter expertise in an area, and then apply this to support the development of comprehensive and robust governance frameworks. You can challenge incumbent ways of thinking, and will take full ownership of driving forward solutions to ensure compliance and clear transparent oversight
  • Ability to understand and identify who users are and what their needs are, based on evidence. You put users first and can manage competing priorities. You understand the importance of analysis to user-centred design and know how to collaborate with professions that are focused on user-centred design (for example, user research, user experience, content, design, business analysis)
  • Knowledge about quality assurance techniques including those used by other analytical professions, and be comfortable deploying these as relevant. You can identify the right data sources and then validate and understand how to interpret the results. You know how to ensure that data is fit for purpose

Alongside your salary of Â£44,004, Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government contributes Â£12,747 towards you being a member of the Civil Service Defined Benefit Pension scheme. Find out what benefits a Civil Service Pension provides (opens in a new window).

At MHCLG we offer many benefits that range from tailored career pathways and flexible working to MyLifestyle Childcare Voucher and Cycle to Work Schemes. For more information, please click here.

Artificial intelligence

Artificial intelligence can be a useful tool to support your application, however, all examples and statements provided must be truthful, factually accurate and taken directly from your own experience. Where plagiarism has been identified (presenting the ideas and experiences of others, or generated by artificial intelligence, as your own) applications may be withdrawn and internal candidates may be subject to disciplinary action. Please see our candidate guidance (opens in a new window) for more information on appropriate and inappropriate use.

Selection process details

Application and Selection

The application process will be split into 2 stages, testing the following Success Profiles:

Experience , Strengths , Technical

Please ensure your CV does not contain any personally identifiable information.

Note: We do not consider direct CV applications. All applications must be submitted via the provided application link.

Important: Your CV and Cover Letter must be merged into a single document before uploading.

Stage 1: Sift (CV & Cover Letter)

Experience: This will be assessed via your CV.

Technical: Your covering letter should be no more than 1 page referencing how you meet the criteria set out in the job description & person specification.

Most of our campaigns utilise multiple assessors and so it is possible that each of your answers would be viewed by different assessors.

Stage 2: Interview

  • Experience: Experience questions will be based around the essential skills and criteria as listed in the job description.
  • Technical: Technical questions will be based around the essential skills and criteria as listed in the job description.
  • Strengths: The strength-based questions will require natural responses from the candidates.

The interview will be of a blended nature consisting of experience, technical and strength-based questions as listed in the advert. The strength-based questions will require natural responses from the candidates. â¯

Sift and Interview Dates

We are a growing organization committed to fostering a professional, inclusive, and employee-focused workplace. Our Human Resources team plays a critical role in supporting our workforce and ensuring that employees receive the resources, guidance, and support needed to succeed. We are currently seeking a highly organized and detail-oriented Human Resources Assistant to join our team and contribute to the smooth operation of our HR department.

The Human Resources Assistant will provide comprehensive administrative and operational support across various HR functions, including recruitment, onboarding, employee relations, record management, compliance, and internal communications. This role is ideal for an individual who enjoys working with people, has strong organizational skills, and is passionate about supporting employee success while maintaining accurate and confidential HR records.

As a key member of the HR team, you will assist with day-to-day human resources activities and help ensure that HR processes are carried out efficiently and in accordance with company policies and employment regulations.

Â 

Key Responsibilities:

Â 

Recruitment & Hiring Support

  • Assist with the recruitment process by posting job advertisements, screening applications, and coordinating interviews.
  • Schedule interviews and communicate with candidates throughout the hiring process.
  • Prepare offer letters, employment contracts, and onboarding documentation.
  • Maintain recruitment records and applicant tracking systems.

Employee Onboarding & Administration

  • Coordinate new employee onboarding and orientation activities.
  • Prepare employee files and ensure all required documentation is completed and maintained.
  • Assist employees with HR-related inquiries and requests.
  • Maintain accurate employee records, databases, and filing systems.

HR Operations & Compliance

  • Ensure employee records remain current, accurate, and compliant with company policies and legal requirements.
  • Assist in monitoring HR procedures and compliance standards.
  • Support the preparation of HR reports, metrics, and documentation.
  • Help administer employee policies, handbooks, and workplace procedures.

Employee Engagement & Support

  • Support employee engagement initiatives, training programs, and company events.
  • Assist with performance review administration and employee development activities.
  • Coordinate internal communications related to HR programs and announcements.
  • Foster positive relationships with employees across all departments.

General Administrative Duties

  • Manage HR correspondence, calendars, and meeting schedules.
  • Prepare reports, presentations, and HR-related documents.
  • Organize and maintain confidential personnel records.
  • Provide administrative support to the HR Manager and wider leadership team as required.

Â 

Qualifications:

  • High school diploma, Associate's or Bachelor's degree.
  • Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint.
  • Ability to maintain confidentiality and handle sensitive information with discretion.
  • Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Professional, approachable, and team-oriented attitude.

Â 

What We Offer:

  • Competitive salary package.
  • Professional development and career advancement opportunities.
  • Supportive and collaborative work environment.
  • Employee training and development programs.
  • Comprehensive onboarding and ongoing support.
  • Opportunity to make a meaningful contribution to employee success and organizational growth.

Â 

If you are passionate about human resources, enjoy supporting people, and thrive in a fast-paced professional environment, we invite you to apply and become an important part of our HR team.

Â

Â 

Key responsibilities:

Â 

Intake & indexing.

  • Receive files from shared inboxes, portals, internal ticketing tools, and cloud folders.
  • Confirm document type, required fields, signatures, and attachments.
  • Index each record using standard metadata (client/project ID, date, document category, version, region).

Â 

Document organization.

  • Apply standardized naming conventions and folder structures.
  • Convert formats when needed (PDF merge/split, image-to-PDF, compressing, version labeling).
  • Maintain controlled libraries (final vs draft vs archived), including retention tags.

Â 

Quality control

  • Perform daily spot checks for duplicates, misfiles, missing pages, and unreadable scans.
  • Flag exceptions and route them to the correct owner with clear notes.
  • Maintain error logs and contribute to process improvements that reduce rework.

Â 

Records governance & compliance

  • Handle sensitive records with strict access controls and confidentiality.
  • Follow retention schedules, legal hold instructions, and deletion/archival rules.
  • Support audits by pulling records quickly and documenting chain-of-custody steps.

Â 

Collaboration & communication.

Coordinate with HR, Finance, Operations, Legal, or Customer teams to clarify file requirements.

Provide status updates on backlog, turnaround time, and issues found during processing.

Â 

Tools you may use:

Â 

  • Cloud storage (Google Drive/SharePoint/Dropbox), document tools (Adobe Acrobat or equivalents), spreadsheets, ticketing (Jira/Asana/ServiceNow), e-signature platforms, and basic office productivity tools.
  • Success metrics (examples):
  • Accuracy rate (misfile/metadata error rate)
  • Turnaround time from receipt to filed
  • Backlog size and daily throughput
  • Audit retrieval speed and completeness
  • Compliance adherence (access control, retention tagging)

Â 

Qualifications:

Â 

  • Experience in records management, admin support, clerical work, or document control (preferred).
  • Strong attention to detail; able to follow naming/filing rules consistently.
  • Comfortable handling confidential information and following strict procedures.
  • Basic computer proficiency: file systems, spreadsheets, PDFs, and collaboration tools.

Â

description:

Work Mode: Remote

As a Virtual Executive Assistant, your primary responsibility is to provide high-level administrative support to executives and leadership teams. Your role is essential in managing schedules, coordinating communications, organizing meetings, and ensuring daily operations run smoothly and efficiently.

You will play a key part in maintaining organization, improving productivity, and supporting important business decisions. Your professionalism, attention to detail, and ability to manage multiple priorities will contribute directly to the success of both our leadership team and the organization as a whole.

title: Product Marketing Manager
Growth Marketing Intern
description:
Growth Marketing Intern - Austin, Fall 2026 (Remote possible)

At GigFinesse, weâre not just changing the game, weâre redefining how live entertainment is booked across the U.S. In just a few years, our platform has powered more than 50,000 shows in 70+ cities, transforming everything from intimate rooftop sessions to marquee venue performances. Our innovative approach has earned recognition from Forbes 30 Under 30, Crainâs Business, and leaders across the hospitality ecosystem.

As we continue to scale, weâre looking for passionate, music-driven individuals to join our team. If youâre excited about building a more accessible, equitable, and reliable platform for artists and venues to connect, create, and thrive, weâd love to meet you.

Position Overview

The Growth Marketing Intern will support initiatives that increase brand awareness, strategic partnerships, and audience growth across GigFinesse and its brands. This role combines market research, partnership development, influencer sourcing, PR support, and digital marketing to help identify new opportunities for growth.

Responsibilities:

title: Marketing Associate
description:
Description

Overview:

Red Rover is seeking an organized, proactive, and eager-to-learn budding marketer or early-career marketing professional to support the execution, coordination, and day-to-day operations of our growing marketing organization as a Marketing Associate.

The Marketing Associate plays an important role in helping ongoing campaigns, programs, and initiatives stay organized, execute successfully, and remain aligned across marketing and cross-functional teams.

This role collaborates across teams, contributing to a wide variety of marketing initiatives and projects. Working alongside experienced marketing leaders, the Marketing Associate will build a broad foundation across campaign marketing, product marketing, content strategy, customer marketing, field marketing and events, sales enablement, and go-to-market execution while helping the team operate more efficiently as Red Rover continues to grow.

The ideal candidate is highly organized, collaborative, and detail-oriented, and enjoys variety, continuous learning, and fast-moving projects. They're eager to gain hands-on experience across all aspects of modern B2B SaaS marketing.

What Youâll Do

Marketing campaign & program support

  • Support the planning, coordination, and execution of integrated marketing campaigns and strategic initiatives across multiple marketing programs
  • Assist with email campaign preparation and scheduling, webinar coordination and preparation, campaign timelines, promotional communications, marketing calendar coordination, campaign asset coordination, website & CRM management, and cross-functional coordination
  • Help ensure campaigns remain organized, on schedule, and aligned across marketing and sales teams

Cross-functional coordination & team support

  • Help coordinate projects, communication, and day-to-day marketing initiatives across sales, SDR, and marketing teams
  • Support activities including project timeline management, meeting preparation, action item tracking, workflow coordination, internal communications, marketing request management, project documentation, team follow-up

Event & field marketing support

  • Support the planning and execution of webinars, conferences, customer events, regional marketing initiatives, and field marketing programs alongside marketing team
  • Assist with webinar event logistics, registration management, presentation and speaker coordination, promotional campaigns and communications, sales coordination, post-event follow-ups, etc.
  • Assist with strategic planning and onsite execution logistics for tradeshows, conferences, and regional events
  • Help contribute to memorable customer and prospect experiences before, during, and after each event

Content & marketing asset coordination

  • Support the marketing organization in publishing and maintaining marketing content library and sales resources across multiple channels
  • Assist with blog publishing, marketing asset organization, webinar materials, sales collateral materials, digital asset management, social media scheduling, website content coordination, and content library management

Marketing operations & team organization

  • Help maintain the operational foundation that keeps the marketing organization running efficiently
  • Support activities including project tracking, campaign organization, campaign performance reporting support, workflow management, budget tracking assistance, tradeshow and events calendar management, etc.
  • Assist in marketing database updates and campaign and program builds alongside marketing operations team

Professional growth & continuous learning

  • Take an active role in developing marketing knowledge and expanding level of responsibilities over time
  • Demonstrate curiosity, initiative, and a willingness to continuously improve and learn while eager to build experience and deepen knowledge across demand generation, campaign marketing, product marketing, brand marketing, content strategy, customer marketing and advocacy, field marketing and events, marketing operations, go-to-market strategy, and AI and traditional or emerging marketing technologies

Requirements

Qualifications:

  • 0-3 years of experience in marketing, communications, business, customer-facing roles, events, or related fields
  • Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field preferred
  • Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail, proven project management skills preferred
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Positive attitude with a willingness to learn and take initiative
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
  • Strong follow-through and reliability
  • Comfortable working cross-functionally with multiple teams
  • Familiarity with Microsoft Office and SharePoint/OneDrive
  • Experience with Canva, HubSpot, or similar marketing platforms and tools is a plus
  • Interest in SaaS, education technology, or B2B marketing environments preferred
  • Ability to travel 15-20% to attend department summits, company events, and regional/sales events

Bonus Skills

  • Experience supporting marketing campaigns, events, or social media initiatives
  • Familiarity with email marketing platforms or marketing automation tools
  • Basic graphic design experience using Canva or Adobe Express
  • Experience with project management platforms such as Asana, Monday.com, or Airtable
  • Experience writing or editing marketing content
  • Interest in AI tools that improve marketing productivity and execution
  • Exposure to CRM platforms such as HubSpot or Salesforce
  • Experience working with K-12 education or other mission-driven organizations

title: Help Desk IT Support Specialist
location: Pasig, Pasig, National Capital Region, Philippines
description:
At Umpisa Inc., our mission is to make the Philippines be known globally as a tech hub.

Umpisa Inc. is a progressive technology services company that partners with select industries, clients and people to work on pioneering and industry-changing solutions via digital transformation, modern software development and venture building.

We create a set of world-class and impactful products and solutions to help organizations and individuals live better lives. We offer demanding, challenging and rewarding careers in software development, product development, emerging technologies, and more for the right candidates.

Essential Skills:

  • Aligns with our values: Excellence, Integrity, Professionalism, People Success, Customer Success, Fun, Innovation and Diversity
  • Strong communication skills
  • Strong problem solving and analytical skills
  • Excellent problem-solving ability
  • Would like to work as part of a self-organizing Scrum team in a scaled agile framework
  • Must be a self-starter and loves to collaborate with the team and client

Job Summary

We are looking for a dedicated and customer-focused Help Desk / IT Support Specialist to provide first-level technical support to internal users. The ideal candidate has 2-5 years of IT support experience, strong troubleshooting skills, and a passion for delivering excellent technical assistance while ensuring a positive user experience.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide first-line technical support for hardware, software, and network-related issues
  • Quickly respond to support requests via ticketing system, email, chat, or phone
  • Troubleshoot and resolve common IT issues (PCs, laptops, printers, applications, connectivity, etc.)
  • Escalate complex issues to appropriate teams when necessary
  • Document issues, resolutions, and procedures clearly and accurately
  • Assist with user account setup, access management, and basic system administration
  • Ensure timely and professional communication with end users
  • Follow IT policies, security standards, and best practices

Requirements

  • At least 2-5 year of experience in a Help Desk, IT Support, or similar roles.
  • Intermediate knowledge of:
    • Windows OS (and/or macOS)
    • Microsoft 365 / Office applications
    • Basic networking and troubleshooting
  • Strong problem-solving and communication skills
  • Customer-oriented mindset with attention to detail
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple support requests

Nice to Have

  • Experience with ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk, etc.)
  • Basic knowledge of Active Directory or user access management
  • IT-related certification is a plus but not required

Work Setup & Schedule

  • Remote set-up
  • Shift schedule: Mid-Shift from 3:00PM onwards
  • Shift flexibility may be considered in the future based on performance and team needs

title: Handy Person
description:
Company Description

Cooinda Lodge Kakadu is a unique resort nestled in the heart of Kakadu National Park, Northern Territory, Australia. Under a Accor's Hotel Management Agreement in partnership with the Djigardaba Enterprise Aboriginal Corporation, it offers guests a gateway to explore the UNESCO World Heritage-listed park. The lodge provides a variety of accommodations, including lodge rooms, eco-tents, and camping sites, along with amenities such as two swimming pools, restaurants, bars, a general store, and access to Yellow Water Cruises and the Warradjan Cultural Centre.

Cooinda Lodge is proudly committed to Indigenous employment, with 25% of our team Bining, working on country. As part of our ongoing support into Indigenous employment and development, we collaborate with the Indigenous Land and Sea Corporation through the Real Jobs Program.

We are an equal opportunity employer, committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, experiences, and identities.

You must have the legal right to work in Australia to be considered for this role.

Job Description

  • Performing daily checks and minor repairs (e.g. fixing leaking taps, replacing lightbulbs, clearing pathways, inspect and clean air conditioning filters, clean and maintain pool filters, etc.).
  • Maintaining guest facilities and lodge equipment (e.g. air-conditioning filter cleaning, pool upkeep,etc.).
  • Supporting scheduled preventive maintenance (e.g. servicing generators, inspecting roofing and water heaters, conduct pest and termite inspection, etc.).

Qualifications

  • 1â2 yearsâ experience in a similar role, with understanding of basic plumbing, electrical, HVAC, and general repair work.
  • Reliable, committed, and adaptable to shift work; ability to multitask and manage time effectively in a dynamic hospitality environment.
  • Good interpersonal skills, integrity, and a strong commitment to guest satisfaction, safety, and Accorâs Heartist values.

Additional Information

Cooinda Lodge Kakadu, offers a unique opportunity to work in one of Australia's most iconic locations, surrounded by natural beauty and rich cultural heritage. We provide staff accommodation and meals on-site, ensuring a comfortable and supportive work environment. Join our team and be part of an unforgettable experience in the heart of Kakadu National Park.

We Offer

  • Subsidised Housing and meals provided
  • Unique work environment surrounded by natural and cultural heritage.

title: Life Guard
description:
Company Description

As a pioneer in the art of responsible hospitality, the Accor Group gathers more than 45 brands, 5,600 hotels, 10,000 restaurants, and lifestyle destinations in 110 countries. While each brand has its own personality, where you will be able to truly find yourself, they all share a common ambition: to keep innovating and challenging the status-quo.

By joining us, you will become a HeartistÂ®, because hospitality is, first and foremost, a work of heart.

You will join a caring environment and a team where you can be all you are. You will be in a supportive place to grow, to fulfil yourself, to discover other professions and to pursue career opportunities, in your hotel or in other hospitality environments, in your country or anywhere in the world!

You will enjoy exclusive benefits, specific to the sector and beyond, as well as strong recognition for your daily commitment.

Everything you will do with us, regardless of your profession, will offer a deep sense of meaning, to create lasting, memorable and impactful experiences for your customers, for your colleagues and for the planet.

Job Description

  • To guard our guests life against drowning in pool or beach
  • Contentious monitoring for hotel pool and beach and make all hotel signs in his area updated.
  • Have a governmental certificate in life guard updated and renew it.
  • Informing his management to prepare reports for any hazard situations during his shift.
  • To be aware and have enough knowledge about first aids and how dealing with drowning cases.
  • To be flexible in assisting around different areas of the hotel and to perform any other tasks assigned by your management.
  • Operates a quality professional fitness program carrying out fitness testing, evaluations, and program design and fitness instruction.
  • To be aware with hotelâs health, safety and hygiene policies.
  • Ensure all safety and cleaning standards are maintained throughout the hotel.
  • To predict that all activities and to be purchased all equipment and belongings may create disease for the environment, to join all activities which is organized to protect biological diversity and shares the responsibilities, shares the individual responsibilities in order to increase environment knowledge and to carry on necessary trainings. To provide minimum consumption currently used all stuffs and equipment (paper, printed out etc.).
  • To implement necessary warnings and departmental trainings in order to save energy inside the facility. To carry on implementing decisions which were taken for saving energy.
  • To implement his responsibilities in order to eliminate and collect waste in a proper way, reduce environmental pollution and harmful effects to the environment.
  • Carry out all responsibilities related to the quality management and food safety management systems implemented at the facility.
  • Carry out all other duties assigned by managers and hotel management not specified in the job description.

Qualifications

  • Education: At least a high school or vocational diploma.
  • Experience: At least 2 years of related work experience
  • Foreign Language: Sufficient level of English and Arabic to communicate effectively with guests and employees.
  • Courses and Training: Professional trainings and seminars and professional certification.
  • Computer Literacy: Not Required
  • Skills: Expected to use basic tools and equipment related to the job. Expected to perform simple, specific and standard tasks that are generally repetitive.

title: Licensed Mental Health Therapist
description:
At LifeStance Health, we believe in a truly healthy society where mental and physical healthcare are unified to make lives better. Our mission is to help people lead healthier, more fulfilling lives by improving access to trusted, affordable, and personalized mental healthcare. Everywhere. Every day. Itâs a lofty goal; we know. But we make it happen with the best team in behavioral health. 

 

Thank you for taking the time to explore a career with us. As the fastest growing behavioral health practice group in the country, now is the perfect time to join our clinical team!

 


We are actively looking to hire a talented remote therapist (LPC, LCSW or LMFT) to support our growing Houston, TX offices, who is passionate about patient care and committed to clinical excellence.

\n


Is this you?
  • Wanting to deliver high quality behavioral healthcare.
  • Seeking work life balance.
  • Interested in growing professionally.


What we offer Therapists:
  • 100% outpatient work in a group practice setting.
  • Generous âabove marketâ compensation with unlimited/uncapped earnings.
  • Full benefits package: health, dental, vision, life, 401k (with match), parental leave, EAP and more.
  • Collegial work environment.
  • Newly designed and modern offices.
  • Full administrative support.
  • Strong work/life balance.
  • Compensation range $75,000-$100,000+compensation model based on productivity
  • Sign on Bonus
  • Annual Cash Bonus Incentive Plan


Licensed Therapists are a critical part of our clinical team. Weâre seeking LCSWs that are:
  • Fully licensed as an LCSW, LPC or LMFT in the State of Texas. We are not able to accommodate dependently licensed clinicians at this time.
  • Experienced in working with adult, and/or child and adolescent populations.
  • This is a Remote Role, candidates must be based in Houston.


\n

About LifeStance Health 

LifeStance is a national provider of mental healthcare services focused on evidenced-based, medically driven treatment services for children, adolescents and adults suffering from a variety of mental health issues in an outpatient care setting, both in-person at its clinics nationwide and through its digital health telemedicine offering. The company employs psychiatrists, psychologists, psychiatric nurse practitioners, and licensed therapists throughout the US.

 

LifeStance Health is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are fully committed to creating an inclusive work environment for all our employees.

 

Our values:

Belonging: We cultivate a space where everyone can show up as their authentic self.

Empathy: We seek out diverse perspectives and listen to learn without judgment.

Courage: We are all accountable for doing the right thing - even when it's hard - because we know it's worth it.

One Team: We realize our full potential when we work together towards our shared purpose.

 

 

 

 

If you elect to interact with us via our website, please only use www.lifestance.com or www.careers.lifestance.com.  Additionally, our recruiters utilize email addresses with the @lifestance.com domain. Other websites and domains are not affiliated with LifeStance Health and may represent threats to your data security.

 

LifeStance Health complies with federal and state disability laws and makes reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees with disabilities. If reasonable accommodation is needed to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and/or to receive other benefits and privileges of employment, please contact our Human Resources Team at  ADA@lifestance.com or by calling +1-800-308-0994. Please note: This contact is intended solely for accommodation requests. Inquiries regarding applications, resumes and applicant status should not be sent to this email address as they will not be reviewed or responded to. To apply for a position, please use our official careers page.

title: Office Admin
description:
Customer Care & Administrative Coordinator (Remote)

TruBlue of Delmarva is seeking an organized, energetic, and customer-focused Customer Care & Administrative Coordinator to join our growing team. This position plays a vital role in ensuring every customer receives exceptional service from their first phone call through project completion.

We're looking for someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, enjoys helping people, and takes pride in staying organized. If you're a self-starter with excellent communication skills and a passion for providing outstanding customer service, we'd love to hear from you.

Responsibilities

Customer Service
- Answer incoming phone calls and respond to customer inquiries professionally and promptly.
- Respond quickly to web leads from Google, Thumbtack, and other marketing sources.
- Schedule estimates and service appointments using Housecall Pro.
- Confirm upcoming appointments and communicate schedule changes with customers.
- Conduct customer satisfaction follow-up calls after completed projects.
- Request Google reviews and maintain positive customer relationships.

Sales & Office Support
- Follow up on outstanding estimates and assist in converting leads into booked jobs.
- Maintain accurate customer records and job notes within Housecall Pro.
- Assist with invoicing, collecting deposits, and tracking customer communications.
- Coordinate calendars for the owner and project managers.
- Support daily office operations and administrative tasks.

Marketing Support
- Coordinate social media content and before-and-after project photos.
- Assist with email marketing campaigns and customer newsletters.
- Help maintain our online reputation by monitoring reviews and customer feedback.

General Administration
- Organize digital files and maintain accurate records.
- Assist with purchasing office supplies and coordinating vendors when needed.
- Continuously improve administrative processes and workflows.
- Perform other administrative and customer service duties as assigned.

Qualifications

- High school diploma or equivalent required.
- Previous experience in customer service, scheduling, administrative support, dispatching, or office management preferred.
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
- Strong organizational and time management abilities.
- Comfortable multitasking in a fast-paced environment.
- Friendly, positive, and professional demeanor.
- Strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills.
- Proficiency with Microsoft Office and Google Workspace.
- Experience with CRM or scheduling software (Housecall Pro experience is a plus but not required).

Preferred Experience

Experience in one or more of the following industries is highly desirable:
- Home services
- Construction
- Property management
- HVAC
- Plumbing
- Electrical
- Restoration
- Insurance or real estate offices

What Success Looks Like

The successful candidate will:
- Answer customer inquiries promptly and professionally.
- Respond to new leads within minutes whenever possible.
- Help keep our schedule organized and efficient.
- Deliver exceptional customer experiences.
- Maintain accurate records and follow-through on every customer interaction.
- Contribute to the continued growth of our company.

About TruBlue of Delmarva

TruBlue of Delmarva is a locally owned, veteran-owned home services company providing handyman, painting, remodeling, and home maintenance services throughout the Delmarva Peninsula. We are committed to delivering exceptional craftsmanship, outstanding customer service, and honest communication on every project.

We believe our employees are our greatest asset. We foster a supportive team environment where initiative, professionalism, and customer satisfaction are recognized and rewarded.

Benefits

- Competitive hourly pay
- Flexible schedule
- Paid training
- Paid time off (eligible employees)
- Opportunities for advancement as the company grows
- Supportive team environment
- Remote position
- Performance-based bonuses and incentives

If you're looking for an opportunity to make a meaningful impact with a growing company that values integrity, teamwork, and exceptional customer service, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Retoucher
location: Buenos Aires, Buenos Aires, Provincia de Buenos Aires, Argentina
description:
Remote position (only for professionals based in Argentina or Uruguay)

RYZ Labs is looking for a Pre-Press Retoucher to join one of our clientâs teams, from the sports merchandising industry. The ideal candidate will translate card designs into beautiful, error-free, and quality-checked forms that are ready for the press. This professional will support imaging, color correction, and retouching of card images/designs in line with the Pre-Press teamâs commitment to excellence.

The ideal candidate is design-savvy and organized, exhibits strong attention to detail, and brings deep experience in imaging and retouching operations. Candidates with a passion for sports and entertainment will likely find themselves a strong cultural fit.

Responsibilities:

  • Ensure all images meet color accuracy and consistency requirements
  • Maintain quality control procedures to ensure that all visual materials (e.g., images, graphics) align with the highest standards of the business and meet the specific requirements of each project
  • Work cross-functionally with photographers, designers, and other stakeholders to achieve desired retouching results for various projects
  • Identify and resolve issues related to image quality, resolution, and/or color accuracy
  • Foster a collaborative, efficient, and communication-first work environment
  • Stay informed on industry best practices, technologies, and software


Requirements:

  • Proven experience (3-5 years) in imaging and retouching operations
  • Proficiency in imaging and retouching software, such as Adobe Photoshop, Lightroom, or other specialized color correction/retouching tools
  • Strong knowledge of color theory and color management
  • Strong organizational skills, communication abilities, and attention to detail


About RYZ Labs:

RYZ Labs is a startup studio built in 2021 by two lifelong entrepreneurs. The founders of RYZ have worked at some of the world's largest tech companies and some of the most iconic consumer brands. They have lived and worked in Argentina for many years and have decades of experience in Latam. What brought them together is the passion for the early phases of company creation and the idea of attracting the brightest talents in order to build industry-defining companies in a post-pandemic world.

Our teams are remote and distributed throughout the US and Latam. They use the latest cutting-edge technologies in cloud computing to create applications that are scalable and resilient. We aim to provide diverse product solutions for different industries, planning to build a large number of startups in the upcoming years.

At RYZ, you will find yourself working with autonomy and efficiency, owning every step of your development. We provide an environment of opportunities, learning, growth, expansion and challenging projects. You will deepen your experience while sharing and learning from a team of great professionals and specialists.

Our values and what to expect:

  • Customer First Mentality - every decision we make should be made through the lens of the customer
  • Bias for Action - urgency is critical, expect that the timeline to get something done is accelerated
  • Ownership - step up if you see an opportunity to help, even if not your core responsibility
  • Humility and Respect - be willing to learn, be vulnerable, and treat everyone that interacts with RYZ with respect
  • Frugality - being frugal and cost-conscious helps us do more with less
  • Deliver Impact - get things done in the most efficient way
  • Raise our Standards - always be looking to improve our processes, our team, our expectations. Status quo is not good enough and never should be
